author: @carlosforma5978
text:
To be fair, sending over the contract in full should be the standard practice from the start.

As in, send the original file, send a commentated file with highlighted important bits and simplified explanations (which help check those for veracity one by one, as opposed to searching through a sea of jargon), and a summary of the main points.

That way, the talent has the option to check and double check the information directly from the start, and point out any discrepancies before moving on with the deal.

Where they find 1 discrepancy there may be a lot more, so you have to get things exactly right. 

With companies, they classically pay for things AFTER they are resolved, not during or before. See the terms Net30, Net60, and  Net90 as well as purchase orders.

Some of these sponsors can cause you problems just by association, so being careful is the right move.
